cross_scene_arcs.title: From Rejection to Self-Acceptance: Ed's Romantic Identity Arc
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ed evolves from seeking external validation through romantic pursuit to achieving genuine connection by embracing his authentic self. Initially mocked and insecure, he later uses wealth to impress, but ultimately wins intimacy by revealing vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ed seeks Muffy's approval through imitation, masking insecurity with performative behavior.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ed shares his nerdy past with Julie and is accepted, achieving mutual attraction through honesty.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite growth, Ed's pattern of tying self-worth to romantic outcomes suggests lingering emotional dependency.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Father-Son Dynamic: Control, Abandonment, and Reconnection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ed's relationship with Mike shifts from fearful detachment to active engagement, then collapses under emotional strain before culminating in painful truth-telling and eventual separation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ed reacts to Mike's transformation with terror, rejecting him as a threat.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ed confronts Mike with the reality of his divorce and urges acceptance, leading to Mike's departure and emotional independence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The lack of closure in their final interaction leaves uncertain whether their bond was repaired or permanently fractured.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Ed's Crisis of Authority: From Detachment to Failed Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ed transitions from passive observer to active authority figure, attempting to reclaim control over Mike and his household, but his efforts culminate in chaotic overreach and symbolic humiliation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ed tries to maintain emotional distance from Mike's high school reentry, resisting involvement.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ed forcibly restores order at the party and asserts dominance, yet is undermined by physical comedy (pizza on head), revealing fragile authority.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ed's need to project control conflicts with his emotional instability, leaving his role as a parent inconsistently defined.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Collapse of Ed's Emotional Facade
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As Ed's marriage dissolves and his influence over Mike wanes, he cycles through denial, impulsive action, and intervention before finally confronting painful reality, marking an internal conflict between avoidance and acceptance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ed confronts Mike about college applications amid divorce papers, revealing suppressed fear of abandonment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ed abandons fantasy and tells Mike the marriage is over, accepting irreversible loss.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ed's own grief remains unprocessed; he helps Mike face reality while avoiding his own emotional reckoning.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Manipulation and Redemption: Ed's Shifting Motivations in Guiding Mike
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ed alternates between using Mike for personal gain and genuinely supporting his growth, reflecting a mixed arc of selfishness and paternal care that peaks during the party crisis when he acts as both enforcer and investigator.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ed encourages Mike to return to school to see the principal, prioritizing romance over Mike's well-being.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ed takes charge during the party, restoring order and initiating a search for Mark Freedman, suggesting a shift toward responsible engagement.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The duality of Ed's intentions—whether he acts for Mike or himself—remains ambiguous, undermining full redemption.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Self-Worth in the Workplace
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mike's professional identity deteriorates as systemic bias undermines his career aspirations, exposing a deepening rift between his contributions and recognition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mike believes his 18 years of service and strong performance will lead to a well-deserved promotion.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He is publicly passed over for a less-qualified candidate due to lacking a college degree, reinforcing his marginalization and internalized inadequacy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The structural inequities in workplace advancement remain unchallenged, leaving Mike questioning whether effort and loyalty matter.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Fatherhood and the Struggle for Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mike's attempts to reconnect with his children evolve from passive presence to active protection, revealing his growing emotional investment despite increasing alienation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mike is emotionally distant and rebuffed by Maggie when she shares her Georgetown acceptance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He commits to re-enrolling in high school and training Alex, transforming from an absent father into a determined guardian.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His children's full acceptance of him remains uncertain, and his unconventional methods risk further destabilizing family dynamics.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Past to Reclaim the Future
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mike's journey from high school glory to adult disillusionment culminates in a physical return to his youth, where he must reconcile past ideals with present purpose to reclaim his future.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: As a high school athlete, Mike is celebrated and seen as a leader with college potential.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Years later, he is rejected by the school community as an adult, but eventually re-enters it as a student with renewed determination to shape his destiny.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Mike can truly change his fate—or if he is doomed to repeat it—remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Mystery of Identity and Lineage
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mike's struggle with identity intensifies as revelations about his parentage and his adoption of a false persona blur the line between self-discovery and self-deception.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mike begins to question his origins after learning he doesn't resemble Ed and hearing Scarlet's comment about his resemblance to her late husband.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He constructs a fake MySpace profile to impersonate Mark Freedman, further entangling himself in a web of identity confusion and potential exposure.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The truth about Mike's biological parentage—and whether he is Mark Freedman or impersonating him—remains unresolved, threatening his sense of self.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Agency in the Face of Transformation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After a shocking physical regression, Mike moves from confusion and rejection toward self-determination, ultimately choosing to redefine his life despite loss and uncertainty.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mike is disoriented and rejected by Ed after regressing to age 17, left vulnerable and alone.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He resolves to 'do it right this time,' applies to college, and eventually severs ties with his old life, embracing independence under a new identity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Mike's use of a false name in departure suggests he has not fully reconciled his past, leaving his emotional closure incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of Unresolved Love
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scarlet's relationship with Mike evolves from eerie familiarity and suspicion to emotional confrontation and eventual recommitment, driven by unresolved history and identity confusion. The arc traces her journey from shock at Mike's resemblance to her missing husband, through growing suspicion over intimate knowledge he shouldn't possess, to a climactic rejection when he claims to be that husband—only to culminate in her choosing to embrace him again, suggesting deep emotional entanglement transcends logic.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scarlet is stunned by Mike's resemblance to her husband, triggering buried emotions and confusion.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She accepts Mike's kiss and agrees to a secret romantic reconnection, symbolizing emotional surrender despite prior resistance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Mike is truly her husband returned, an imposter, or a symbolic reincarnation remains ambiguous, leaving the authenticity of their bond in question.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Claiming Independence and Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scarlet transitions from emotional passivity to active self-determination, reclaiming agency over her personal, maternal, and professional life. This arc follows her legal divorce, emotional vulnerability with Mike, public affirmation of her creative talent, defiance of Mike's control, and finalization of independence—marking a transformation from a woman defined by absence and duty to one asserting autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scarlet is enduring a failing marriage in silence, emotionally strained and disconnected.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She legally severs ties with her husband, embraces her identity as an artist and independent woman, and prioritizes her own happiness.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Balancing her newfound freedom with her responsibilities as a mother remains a lingering challenge, especially as her children grow more independent.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Push and Pull of Intimacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scarlet and Mike's dynamic oscillates between professional collaboration, flirtation, boundary-setting, and emotional surrender. This arc charts the escalating tension as physical and emotional intimacy grows, only to be repeatedly checked by Scarlet's resistance—until she ultimately reverses course and re-enters the relationship in secrecy, revealing the internal conflict between desire and self-protection.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scarlet hires Mike for a job, initiating a controlled, professional relationship.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She actively reciprocates intimacy and agrees to conceal their renewed romance, signaling a shift from resistance to acceptance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The decision to keep the relationship hidden introduces future risk of betrayal or emotional fallout, especially with their children involved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Ghosts of the Past
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scarlet grapples with the lingering emotional and psychological impact of her husband's disappearance and Mike's uncanny connection to him. This arc weaves together her confrontation with Mike's victim mentality, his evasion of truth, the discovery of a tribute website tied to her past, and the emotional disruption caused by a mysterious letter—each moment peeling back layers of grief, guilt, and unresolved closure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scarlet challenges Mike's passive resentment, revealing her frustration with emotional stagnation in their history.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is emotionally moved by the 'Scarlet's Sanctuaries' website and destabilized by the courthouse letter, suggesting she is beginning to process buried trauma.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true origin and meaning of the website and the letter remain unclear, leaving open whether these are genuine attempts at reconciliation or manipulative gestures.
